content.text:
Good observations — let me answer each, then suggest how to handle the changes.

**What "up-to-date" means.** After fetching, sync classifies each repo's local bookmark against its remote counterpart (`main` vs `main@origin`). "All bookmarks up-to-date" means local and remote point at the same commit in every repo — nothing to pull, nothing to push. The other classifications are `behind` (remote moved → sync fast-forwards), `ahead` (you have unpushed commits → nothing to pull, not an error), and `diverged` (both moved → sync rebases). Erroring when not up-to-date would turn sync back into a validator — acting on behind/diverged *is* sync's job (that error-on-action behavior is exactly the deprecated `--check` mode). But if what you want is quieter output — say nothing when nothing was done, speak only when something changed — that's a reasonable output-shape change, separate from erroring.

**The unconditional `jj new main` on `.claude`.** You're right, and I think you've spotted a real wart. `reposition_session` today always runs `jj new main`, even when `@` is already a child of the current `main` tip. Consequences:

- **Empty `@`, main unmoved** (your vc-template-x1 case): pure churn — jj abandons the empty `@` and mints a fresh one with a new change ID, growing the op log every sync for no benefit.
- **Non-empty `@`, main unmoved** (this workspace's dogfood earlier): worse than churn — the live session writes get shoved onto a sibling head off the main line, and the working copy starts over empty. Any ochid already captured against that `@`'s chid now points at a sibling that the next push won't commit.

The fix mirrors what `reposition_code` already does: **no-op when `@-` is already the `main` tip**, and only `jj new main` when `main` actually moved. That also makes the scenario-2a test expectation change (trailing writes would stay in `@` instead of becoming a sibling head — which I'd argue is the better behavior anyway).

**How to proceed:** the reposition fix touches `reposition_session` in sync.rs — adjacent to but distinct from the bookmark-pinning work in the current `0.68.0-1` commit. Options:

1. Fold it into `0.68.0-1` (same file, small, ships now).
2. Insert it as its own work commit (`0.68.0-2`, pushing the push-side work to `-3`) — cleaner history.
3. New `## Todo` entry, handled in its own cycle along with the quiet-output question.

I'd lean toward option 2. Which would you like?

message.content: new -2 is the right call

content.text:
Agreed — a fully clean sync should say nothing. My proposal for the output shape:

- **Silent (demoted to `debug!`)**: the `sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date` summary, `@ already on '<bookmark>'` (code repo), and `@ already on 'main'` (session repo).
- **Still visible (`info!`)**: anything that mutates state (fast-forward, rebase, `jj new`), the `leaving @ in place` cases (sync chose *not* to act on a non-trivial `@` — worth knowing), `no remote counterpart — skipping`, and the fetch/classify report when action is needed.
- **Errors**: unchanged, loud.

So `vc-x1 sync` with nothing to do prints nothing and exits 0; `-v` still shows the details.

That's a separate concern from the `reposition_session` fix sitting uncommitted, so I'd commit `0.68.0-2` first and do the quiet output as a new `0.68.0-3` (push work slides to `-4`). Here's the `-2` description:

**Title:**
```
fix: sync session reposition no-op (0.68.0-2)

```
reposition_session ran jj new main unconditionally: an empty @
churned its chid/op every sync, and a non-empty @'s live
session writes — plus any ochid captured against its chid —
were stranded on a sibling head. Now it no-ops when @- is
already the main tip; jj new main only runs when main moved.

- Cargo.toml: version 0.68.0-1 → 0.68.0-2

  sync_session_noop_when_up_to_date — @ keeps its change id,
  trailing writes stay in the working copy, no sibling head

content.text:
No existing probe — clone just attempts both and dies on the second. Here's my thinking:

**The wrinkle**: falling back to single-repo on *any* `.claude` clone failure would mask real errors (network hiccup, auth). The clean way is to probe first: after the code repo clones, run `git ls-remote <session-url> HEAD` — cheap, no side effects. If the probe fails, there's no companion repo (or no access — GitHub deliberately reports both identically), so skip the bot clone and symlink and report it plainly. If the probe succeeds, clone it, and any failure after that is a genuine error that still stops.

**Resulting behavior** for your case:

```
Cloning git@github.com:winksaville/zed-markdown-link-issue → .../zmli...
no companion session repo at git@github.com:winksaville/zed-markdown-link-issue.claude — cloning code repo only

Done! Cloned single repo to .../zmli
  Code repo: .../zmli
```

i.e. same result as `--por`, arrived at gracefully. `--por` stays as the explicit "don't even look for a companion" switch, and the dry-run text gains a note that step 2/3 are skipped when no companion exists.

**Plan**: new work commit `0.68.0-4` (push work slides to `-5`): `remote_exists` helper (probably in `url.rs` or `common.rs`), `clone_dual` probes + falls back with distinct done-message, integration test with a local fixture (code bare repo present, no `.claude` bare repo — the path form makes this easy to test offline).

Want me to proceed with that as `-4`?

1	//! `push` subcommand — collapse the dual-repo commit+push+finalize
2	//! ceremony into a single resumable command.
3	//!
4	//! See `notes/chores/chores-05.md > Add push subcommand (0.37.0)` for the
5	//! full design.
6	//!
7	//! Dev-step ladder (expanded from original 4 to 6 after adding an
8	//! integration-test step ahead of the first dogfood):
9	//!
10	//! - `0.37.0-0` — scaffolding: flag surface, `Stage` enum, stub `push()`
11	//! - `0.37.0-1` — state file + stage-dispatch loop with stage stubs;
12	//!   `--status`, `--restart`, `--from`
13	//! - `0.37.0-2` — real stage bodies (commits, bookmarks, push,
14	//!   finalize) + `jj op` snapshot rollback
15	//! - `0.37.0-3` — integration tests + workspace-root refactor
16	//!   (thread `root: &Path` through every stage so fixtures can
17	//!   point them at tempdirs); first `vc-x1 push` dogfood ships
18	//!   this commit
19	//! - `0.37.0-4` — interactivity: two approval gates, `$EDITOR`,
20	//!   message persistence across resumes
21	//! - `0.37.0-5` — polish: `--dry-run`, `--step`, non-tty handling,
22	//!   `.gitignore` coherence warning
23	//! - `0.37.0` — docs + workflow migration (done marker)

27	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

37	use crate::toml_simple::toml_load;

39	/// Bookmark the session (`.claude`) repo always advances and
40	/// pushes. The session repo is a linear journal on `main` by
41	/// design — `<bookmark>` names a code-repo bookmark only.
42	const SESSION_BOOKMARK: &str = "main";
43	
44	/// Named stages of the `push` state machine.
45	///
46	/// Used by `--from <stage>` to resume at a specific point and by
47	/// `--status` to report the current position. Ordered top-down so
48	/// `Stage as u8` comparisons reflect progress through the flow.
49	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ValueEnum)]
50	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
51	pub enum Stage {
52	    /// Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest.
53	    Preflight,
54	    /// Present diff for the first approval gate.
55	    Review,
56	    /// Compose / edit the commit message; present for second gate.
57	    Message,
58	    /// Commit the app repo.
59	    CommitApp,
60	    /// Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if empty).
61	    CommitClaude,
62	    /// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: app → `<bookmark>`,
63	    /// session → `main`. Renamed from `bookmark-both` (0.68.0) when
64	    /// the two repos stopped advancing the same name; the old name
65	    /// is kept as a parse alias.
66	    #[value(alias = "bookmark-both")]
67	    BookmarkSet,
68	    /// `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`.
69	    PushApp,
70	    /// `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push main ...`.
71	    FinalizeClaude,
72	}
73	
74	impl Stage {
75	    /// Return the stage's kebab-case string identifier (matches what
76	    /// the CLI accepts via `--from <stage>` and what `PushState`
77	    /// persists to disk).
78	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
79	        match self {
80	            Stage::Preflight => "preflight",
81	            Stage::Review => "review",
82	            Stage::Message => "message",
83	            Stage::CommitApp => "commit-app",
84	            Stage::CommitClaude => "commit-claude",
85	            Stage::BookmarkSet => "bookmark-set",
86	            Stage::PushApp => "push-app",
87	            Stage::FinalizeClaude => "finalize-claude",
88	        }
89	    }
90	
91	    /// Parse a kebab-case stage name back into a `Stage`.
92	    ///
93	    /// Unknown names return `None`; callers should surface a helpful
94	    /// error rather than silently substituting a default.
95	    /// `bookmark-both` is accepted as a legacy alias for
96	    /// `bookmark-set` (pre-0.68.0 state files).
97	    pub fn from_str(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
98	        match name {
99	            "preflight" => Some(Stage::Preflight),
100	            "review" => Some(Stage::Review),
101	            "message" => Some(Stage::Message),
102	            "commit-app" => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
103	            "commit-claude" => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
104	            "bookmark-set" | "bookmark-both" => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
105	            "push-app" => Some(Stage::PushApp),
106	            "finalize-claude" => Some(Stage::FinalizeClaude),
107	            _ => None,
108	        }
109	    }
110	
111	    /// The stage that follows this one, or `None` when this is the
112	    /// last stage (`FinalizeClaude`).
113	    pub fn next(self) -> Option<Self> {
114	        match self {
115	            Stage::Preflight => Some(Stage::Review),
116	            Stage::Review => Some(Stage::Message),
117	            Stage::Message => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
118	            Stage::CommitApp => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
119	            Stage::CommitClaude => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
120	            Stage::BookmarkSet => Some(Stage::PushApp),
121	            Stage::PushApp => Some(Stage::FinalizeClaude),
122	            Stage::FinalizeClaude => None,
123	        }
124	    }
125	
126	    /// The first stage in the flow — used when no saved state exists.
127	    pub fn first() -> Self {
128	        Stage::Preflight
129	    }
130	}
131

133	///
134	/// Flag set mirrors the design in `notes/chores/chores-05.md`. Flags are
135	/// parsed in 0.37.0-0; their real effects land across the remaining
136	/// 0.37.0-N steps. See the module docstring for which flags activate
137	/// when.
138	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
139	pub struct PushArgs {
140	    /// Bookmark to advance in the code repo (positional form of

143	    /// Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`

152	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
153

193	    #[arg(short = 'y', long)]
194	    pub yes: bool,
195	}

225	    #[command(long_about = "Squash, set bookmark, and/or push a jj repo.\n\n\
226	        Designed for the bot to atomically finalize its session repo:\n\
227	        --detach exits immediately, --delay waits for trailing writes,\n\

232	    /// Dual-repo commit+push+finalize in one resumable command
233	    #[command(
234	        long_about = "Dual-repo commit+push+finalize in one resumable command.\n\n\
235	        Collapses today's manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow into a\n\
236	        single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a\n\
237	        state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can\n\
238	        resume without re-doing completed stages.\n\n\
239	        Stages: preflight (fmt/clippy/test) → review (approve diff)\n\
240	        → message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →\n\
241	        commit-app → commit-claude (skipped if clean) → bookmark-both\n\
242	        → push-app → finalize-claude. Failures in commit-app /\n\
243	        commit-claude / bookmark-both roll both repos back via\n\
244	        `jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-app.\n\
245	        After push-app succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and\n\
246	        recovery is forward-only.\n\n\
247	        Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes\n\
248	        to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body\n\
249	        across resumes so only the first invocation needs them."
250	    )]
251	    Push(push::PushArgs),
252	}

255	/// in both repos of the dual-repo workspace. Emits one line on
256	/// entry and one on exit of every command. If entry and exit
257	/// differ, the executing command is the culprit; if entry differs
258	/// from the previous command's exit, something *between*
259	/// invocations broke it. Originally added in 0.37.2 as a temporary
260	/// diagnostic; promoted to permanent in 0.37.4 after the user

1	//! Integration tests for the push module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `push_in` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures (bare-git remotes + colocated jj repos under a
5	//! unique tempdir via `crate::test_helpers::Fixture`).
6	//!
7	//! Every test uses `--from message` to skip `preflight` (no
8	//! `Cargo.toml` in the fixture) and `--no-finalize` to avoid
9	//! spawning a detached `vc-x1 finalize` child that would
10	//! outlive the test. The remaining stages (message,
11	//! commit-app, commit-claude, bookmark-set, push-app) are
12	//! exercised against the fixture's local bare-git remote.
13	//!
14	//! Stage execution + rollback are covered here;
15	//! state-file / layout / stage-ordering mechanics are covered
16	//! in the neighboring `tests` module via pure unit tests.
17	//!
18	//! Requires `jj` and the compiled `vc-x1` binary in `PATH`.
19	
20	use super::*;
21	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
22	use std::fs;
23	use std::process::Command;
24	
25	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and return trimmed stdout on success.
26	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
27	    let out = Command::new("jj")
28	        .args(args)
29	        .arg("-R")
30	        .arg(repo)
31	        .output()
32	        .expect("spawn jj");
33	    assert!(
34	        out.status.success(),
35	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
36	        repo.display(),
37	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
38	    );
39	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
40	}
41	
42	/// Commit ID (short, 12 chars) for a revision.
43	fn cid(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
44	    jj(
45	        repo,
46	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
47	    )
48	}
49	
50	/// Full description of a revision.
51	fn description(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
52	    jj(repo, &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "description"])
53	}
54	
55	/// First line of a revision's description.
56	fn desc_first_line(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
57	    jj(
58	        repo,
59	        &[
60	            "log",
61	            "-r",
62	            rev,
63	            "--no-graph",
64	            "-T",
65	            "description.first_line()",
66	        ],
67	    )
68	}

73	fn test_params(title: &str, body: &str) -> PushParams {
74	    PushParams {
75	        bookmark: Some("main".to_string()),
76	        restart: false,
77	        from: Some(Stage::Message),
78	        step: false,
79	        status: false,
80	        recheck: false,
81	        no_finalize: true,
82	        dry_run: false,

84	        body: Some(body.to_string()),
85	        yes: true,
86	    }
87	}

90	/// commit lands with an `ochid` trailer pointing at `.claude`'s

92	/// `bookmark-set` + `push-app` still run cleanly.
93	#[test]
94	fn push_happy_claude_clean() {
95	    let fx = Fixture::new("push-clean");

97	
98	    let claude_main_before = cid(&fx.claude, "main");

101	
102	    // App repo: main advanced to our new commit.
103	    assert_eq!(desc_first_line(&fx.work, "main"), "feat: clean case");
104	    let app_full = description(&fx.work, "main");

133	    assert_eq!(desc_first_line(&fx.claude, "main"), "feat: paired change");
134

136	    let app_full = description(&fx.work, "main");
137	    let claude_full = description(&fx.claude, "main");
138	    assert!(

140	        "app ochid missing:\n{app_full}"
141	    );

149	    );
150

152	    assert_ne!(

157	}

Work commit `0.68.0-4` (push side) is complete — and that wraps the backfilling of the pin into push:

- `src/push.rs`:
  - New `SESSION_BOOKMARK` const (`"main"`); the session repo's side of every stage now uses it: preflight tracking, the bookmark stage, `finalize --push main`, and completion-sanity check 3.
  - `bookmark-both` renamed to `bookmark-set` (it now advances *different* names: app → `<bookmark>`, session → `main`). The old spelling survives as a legacy alias in both `Stage::from_str` (pre-0.68.0 state files) and the clap `--from` value.
  - `PushState.bookmark` stays a single code-side field; arg docs say the positional/`--bookmark` is code-repo-only.
- `src/main.rs`: push `long_about` stage list updated.
- Tests: new integration test `push_feature_bookmark_pins_session_to_main` — a `feature`-bookmark push creates and pushes `feature` in the app repo while `.claude` advances only `main` and grows no `feature` bookmark; plus a legacy-alias parse test. (First run of the new test failed on my own assertion — `contains("feature")` matched the commit title — tightened to match bookmark names.)
- `notes/cycle-protocol.md` + `notes/todo.md`: live `--from bookmark-both` references updated to `bookmark-set`.

All 391 tests pass, clippy clean, installed, and the dry-run above shows the renamed stage setting `main` per-repo. Remaining in the ladder: just the `0.68.0` close-out.

Ready to commit — please review.
